title: Ingot scheme-registry declarations
description: Declares the code schemes a deployment speaks: class (identity vs attribute), bridge grade, normalizer, checksum rule, equivalence family. Derived from CodeRegistry's table semantics and Codes' canonicalization — see docs/CLAIMS_CONTRACT.md. Unknown schemes on the wire are still accepted (opaque, non-bridging); a registry extends coverage, it never gates submission.

schemeName.description: Canonical engine-native scheme name — the string before the colon in 'scheme:value'. Lowercase snake_case (cnk, gtin, cip_acl7, …).

class.description: How clustering uses the code: 'identity' bridges listings; 'external_ref' identifies the thing in another system and NEVER bridges; 'attribute' is a non-identifying classification; 'entity_id' is the source system's own record id — not a code claim at all.

bridge_grade.description: The over-merge guard's axis, orthogonal to class: a merge bridged by a 'national' code is trusted; one bridged solely by a 'barcode'-grade code (reusable/reassignable GS1 codes) is suspect and gated; 'none' (default) is not a bridge.

normalizer.description: Canonicalization rule applied to every ingested value. Default when absent: trim.

width.description: All-digit values shorter than this left-pad with zeros (e.g. cip_acl7: 7, pzn: 8, cn: 6).

kind.description: GTIN-family canonicalization: 8/12/13/14-digit values zero-fill to GTIN-14; non-GTIN-shaped values pass through untouched.

checksum.description: Validity rule for validators: 'gtin_mod10' is the GS1 mod-10 check digit. The engine does not enforce checksums at the submission boundary today.

equivalence_family.description: Schemes sharing a family denote the same code space at different widths/spellings; after normalization their values compare equal. Precedent: the GTIN family (ean/upc/eanGtin8-14 fold to gtin).

aliases.description: Alternative scheme spellings accepted on the wire that fold to `name` (e.g. 'ean' and 'upc' for gtin).
